name: Maunawili Falls
tagline: A 20-ft jungle cascade with a swimming hole and cliff-jump rock — best legal swim-at-falls on the windward side.
parking: Very limited street parking at end of Kelewina St — arrive before 8am
bestTime: Early morning before 8am to get parking and beat crowds
talkStory: The swimming hole at these falls is one of the few on Oahu where swimming is relatively tolerated — locals have been jumping off the rock ledge here for decades, though leptospirosis risk exists in all Hawaii freshwater. The trail passes through land that was once part of the Maunawili sugar plantation, and you can spot remnants of old irrigation channels along the stream. In 2018 severe flooding damaged sections of the trail, prompting significant repair work. Arrive before 8am for parking on tiny Kelewina Street — it's a neighborhood, so be respectful.
